events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need parse task. Scenario: missing surge protection on field side, rough-complete, Instrumentation & Controls loop checks & wiring. We must identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because non-compliant (missing surge protection likely required by drawings/standards). Risk maybe medium/high? Missing surge protection can damage instruments, safety/control system, maybe medium or high. We'll choose high? Let's evaluate. Industrial I&C: surge protection devices (SPD) protect field instruments/control loops from transients. Missing on field side violates loop drawings, ISA 5.1? ISA 5.1 is instrumentation symbols and identification, not SPD specifically. Loop drawings specify SPD. Component condition installed-defective? Actually missing component not installed. But category options include installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Missing would be non-compliant. Since component absent, condition categories ["non-compliant"] maybe also installed-defective? installed-defective implies a component installed but defective. Missing is not installed-defective. Use non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete but missing SPD means loop not fully complete; estimate 85%? value 0 no numeric reading. sound_source irrelevant default empty string? Schema says string, maybe "". confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: "Surge protection device omitted on field side per loop drawings", "Control loop vulnerable to voltage transients and EMI". actions: "Install specified surge protection device at field termination/loop boundary", "Verify clamping voltage and grounding per manufacturer and drawing", "Re-perform loop check after installation". rationale: brief. references: "loop drawings", "ISA 5.1". Need raw JSON without markdown. Ensure valid. No extra whitespace? Fine.

Check required: If work dangerous or non-compliant, decision fail. Non-compliant due missing surge protection. So fail. Risk high maybe because can cause instrument/control failure. Could be critical? Missing surge protection in industrial control could cause PLC damage, process upset. But baseline high. We'll choose high.

Need write to /app/answer.json? We are model; final response raw JSON. System says return exactly one valid JSON object matching answer schema. Do not include prose. So output JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Surge protection device omitted on the field side of the control loop",
    "Loop as-built does not match the loop drawing requirements",
    "Field wiring/terminations exposed to transient overvoltage risk"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install the specified surge protection device at the field-side termination location",
    "Bond/ground the SPD per manufacturer and project grounding details",
    "Re-perform the loop check and continuity/insulation verification after SPD installation"
  ],
  "rationale": "Loop drawings require surge protection on the field side; its absence is a non-compliant installation that leaves instrumentation and control loops unprotected from transients, so the loop cannot pass inspection.",
  "references": [
    "loop drawings",
    "ISA 5.1"
  ]
}
